title: Leonhardia escayracii
taxonomy: Leonhardia escayracii is a genus of green algae in the class Chlorophyceae. This genus is typically classified within the phylum Chlorophyta. The classification of Leonhardia escayracii at the family level is within the family Hydrodictyaceae. At the order level, it falls under the order Tetrasporales. Its classification at the kingdom level places it within the kingdom Plantae.

intro: Leonhardia escayracii, also known as Leonhardia, is a plant species in the family Gentianaceae. It has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and enhance overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Leonhardia escayracii include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Leonhardia is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in modern herbalism to support respiratory health and boost the immune system.
